message.content: This parent section (pages 11–12) establishes a 2025 DianDian Data evaluation framework for ten typical anime-style mobile games, asserting that no single title dominates all assessment dimensions and identifying graphics and gameplay as the primary evaluation criteria. **Topics** span market performance, user demographics, core gameplay mechanics, monetization strategies, cross-platform optimization, and community dynamics across **Honkai: Star Rail**, **Genshin Impact**, **Arknights**, **Zenless Zone Zero**, **Blue Archive**, **Goddess of Victory: NIKKE**, **Love and Deepspace**, **Onmyoji**, **Wuthering Waves**, and **Azur Lane**. **Entities** include the reviewed titles, developers/publishers (e.g., miHoYo, Shift Up), platforms (iOS, Android, PC, Console, Steam), and the research source DianDian Data (©2025). **Dates** consistently reference 2025 tracking windows (January–October for AppStore revenue and iOS MAU; Q1–Q3 for retention metrics), alongside game-specific milestones (e.g., Genshin Impact’s ~2020 launch, Onmyoji’s ~9-year operation, Azur Lane’s 8th anniversary). **Metrics** feature quantitative market data (revenue scales, MAU trends, next-day retention ~21.8%–63.2%, monthly retention ~3.4%–21.4%, gender ratios, user interest categories) and a standardized weighted scoring rubric (Visual/Audio 25%, Core Combat 30%, Content/Systems 20%, Operations/Community 15%, Performance/Device 10%), producing comprehensive scores from 84.2 to 87.8/100. **Claims** highlight industry benchmarks in technical optimization and artistic presentation, the retention impact of stable update cadences and F2P-friendly economies, and recurring pain points including late-game repetition, optimization bottlenecks, gacha fatigue, and localization adjustments. **Definitions and Procedures** detail genre-specific concepts and workflows: "长草期" (content drought), "集成战略" (roguelike mode), "合轴" (synergy trigger), "声骸" (echo progression), "饭圈化" (fandom polarization), "Companionship" design, Burst combat loops, Synchronizer progression, gacha pity/guarantee structures, character progression pipelines (gacha → star-up → material farming), and combat input/optimization steps. **Evidence Types** comprise DianDian Data’s independent market research, supported by comparative charts/tables for comprehensive scores, time-series line/bar graphs for financial and active user trends, retention/demographic statistics, ASO keyword coverage lists, and structured qualitative analyses of gameplay design, monetization models, and community feedback. The methodology explicitly defines "typical products" as expert-curated selections based on market popularity and player attention, clarifying that rankings do not represent an overall market TOP 10.
message.reasoning_content:
Here's a thinking process:

1.  **Analyze User Input:**
    - **Parent Section:** "02典型二次元移动游戏体验测评" (02 Typical Anime-Style Mobile Game Experience Evaluation), pages 11-12.
    - **Child Summaries:** 16 summaries covering various games (Honkai: Star Rail, Genshin Impact, Arknights, Zenless Zone Zero, Blue Archive, Goddess of Victory: NIKKE, Love and Deepspace, Onmyoji, Wuthering Waves, Azur Lane) plus an introductory/comparative section.
    - **Task:** Create a retrieval-oriented summary for the parent section using the child summaries. Mention topics, entities, dates, tables, figures, definitions, procedures, claims, metrics, and evidence types when visible. Do not invent facts. Return only the summary text.

2.  **Identify Key Components from Child Summaries:**
    - *Node 0016:* Intro/Comparison. Topics: No single game dominates all dimensions; graphics & gameplay are most critical. Entities: DianDian Data, 2025. Definitions: "Typical products" curated by experts based on popularity/attention; not overall market TOP 10. Metrics: Multi-dimensional comprehensive scoring. Evidence: Independent research, expert curation. Figures: Comparative chart/table of scores (pp. 12-13).
    - *Node 0017 & 0018:* Honkai: Star Rail. Dates: Launch Apr 2023, data Jan-Oct 2025 (revenue), Jan-Sep 2025 (MAU), Q1-Q3 2025 (retention), v3.2/v3.4 in 2025. Metrics: Revenue, MAU, retention (~48.9% D1, ~17.6% M1), male ratio ~67.9%, overall score 87.8/100 (weights: Visuals 25%/90, Combat 30%/85, Content 20%/87, Ops 15%/86, Perf 10%/92). Gacha: 0.6% base, 160-pull hard pity. Claims: Turn-based strategy depth, Roguelite replayability, consistent updates sustain vitality. Evidence: Trend charts, stats, qualitative analysis.
    - *Node 0024 & 0025:* Genshin Impact. Dates: Launched ~2020, data Jan-Oct 2025, Q1-Q3 2025. Metrics: Revenue, MAU, retention (~56.5% D1, ~21.4% M1), male ratio ~68.5%. Claims: Industry benchmark for ACG/mobile, top-tier AV, elemental combat, cross-platform sync. Evidence: Charts, descriptive text. Definition: "长草期" (content drought).
    - *Node 0031 & 0032:* Arknights. Dates: >7 years operational, data Jan-Oct 2025, Q1-Q3 2025. Metrics: Revenue, MAU, retention (~50.6% D1, ~15.3% M1), male ratio ~79.6%, overall score 86.8/100 (weights: Combat 30%/85, Visuals 25%/90, Content 20%/82, Ops 15%/85, Perf 10%/88). Claims: Strong gameplay/art compensate for flaws (cryptic story, low drop rates, time-consuming). Definitions: "集成战略" (Integrated Strategy/roguelike), "音律联觉" (Ambience Synesthesia concert). Evidence: Charts, scoring rubrics.
    - *Node 0038 & 0039:* Zenless Zone Zero. Dates: Data Jan-Oct 2025, Q1-Q3 2025. Metrics: Revenue, MAU, retention (~24.4% D1, ~3.4% M1), male ratio ~68.1%, overall score 86.6/100. Claims: High production, trendy comic style, accessible/deep combat, rapid patches for criticized mechanics. Definitions: "Hollows", "bullet time", "grid-based" gameplay. Procedures: Combat inputs, performance optimization steps. Evidence: Charts, qualitative analysis.
    - *Node 0045 & 0046:* Blue Archive. Dates: Data Jan-Oct 2025, Q1-Q3 2025. Metrics: Revenue, MAU, retention (~63.2% D1, ~18.6% M1), male ratio ~69.1%, Steam rating 93%, overall score 86.2/100. Claims: T1 cel-shaded art & narrative, stable ops, auto-heavy combat lacks depth, late-game repetition. Definitions: Cel-shaded style, cost-based skill system, dynamic cover. Procedures: Gacha → star-up → weapon upgrade. Evidence: Charts, platform data, scoring.
    - *Node 0052 & 0053:* Goddess of Victory: NIKKE. Dates: Data May-Oct 2025 (revenue), Jul-Oct 2025 (MAU), Q1-Q3 2025. Metrics: Revenue, MAU, retention (~23.3% D1, ~3.6% M1), male ratio ~81.7%, overall score 85.5/100. Claims: Distinctive Kim Hyung-tae art, third-person cover shooter, narrative tension, Synchronizer eases grinding, CN localization impacted some players. Definitions/Procedures: Burst combat loop, Synchronizer system, 200-pull event pity. Evidence: Charts, developer background, system rules.
    - *Node 0059 & 0060:* Love and Deepspace. Dates: Data Jan-Oct 2025, Q1-Q3 2025. Metrics: Revenue, MAU, retention (~24.8% D1, ~3.4% M1), female ratio ~75.4%. Claims: Full 3D otome benchmark, first-person action-romance, high customization, fandom dynamics impact acquisition. Definitions: "Companionship" design, "T0 level", "饭圈化" (fandom behavior). Procedures: Gacha, team comp rules, daily interactions. Evidence: Charts, qualitative analysis.
    - *Node 0066 & 0067:* Onmyoji. Dates: ~9 years operational, data Jan-Oct 2025, Q1-Q3 2025. Metrics: Revenue, MAU, retention (~22.1% D1, ~4.8% M1), male ratio ~74.8%. Claims: High-quality Japanese aesthetics, deep turn-based strategy, sustainable F2P cycle, pain points: Soul RNG, templated events, high onboarding barrier. Definitions/Procedures: Soul matching, turn-based timing, free UR path. Evidence: Charts, ASO keywords, qualitative assessment.
    - *Node 0073 & 0074:* Wuthering Waves. Dates: Data Jan-Oct 2025, Q1-Q3 2025. Metrics: Revenue, MAU, retention (~31.9% D1, ~4.9% M1), male ratio ~83.1%, overall score 84.6/100. Claims: ACT combat benchmark (parry/dodge/aerial), smooth exploration but bland world, resource-intensive progression, mixed banner criticism, optimization issues. Definitions: "合轴" (concerto/synergy trigger), "声骸" (echo progression). Evidence: Charts, gameplay analysis.
    - *Node 0080 & 0081:* Azur Lane. Dates: 8th anniversary, data Jan-Oct 2025, Q1-Q3 2025. Metrics: Revenue, MAU, retention (~21.8% D1, ~3.9% M1), male ratio ~79.9%, overall score 84.2/100. Claims: F2P/micro-spend friendly, skin monetization, dual-stick bullet-hell, "electronic bonsai" progression, stable community. Definitions/Procedures: Ship-type counters, bullet-hell dodging, auto-battle/QoL setups. Evidence: Charts, qualitative analysis.

3.  **Synthesize into a Retrieval-Oriented Parent Summary:**
    - *Structure:* Start with overarching scope/purpose. Then group by common themes (methodology, metrics, game-specific evaluations, evidence types). Ensure all requested elements (topics, entities, dates, tables/figures, definitions, procedures, claims, metrics, evidence) are explicitly mentioned where applicable.
    - *Scope:* Covers 10 typical anime-style mobile games evaluated by DianDian Data in 2025.
    - *Methodology/Intro:* No single title dominates all dimensions; graphics and gameplay are primary assessment criteria. "Typical products" are expert-curated based on market popularity/player attention, not a strict market TOP 10.
    - *Metrics & Data:* Consistent tracking across Jan–Oct 2025 (AppStore revenue, iOS MAU) and Q1–Q3 2025 (next-day & monthly retention, gender demographics, user interests). Each game receives a weighted comprehensive score (out of 100) across five dimensions: Visual/Audio (25%), Core Combat/Gameplay (30%), Content/Systems (20%), Operations/Commercialization/Community (15%), and Performance/Device Adaptation (10%).
    - *Entities/Games:* Honkai: Star Rail, Genshin Impact, Arknights, Zenless Zone Zero, Blue Archive, Goddess of Victory: NIKKE, Love and Deepspace, Onmyoji, Wuthering Waves, Azur Lane. Source: DianDian Data (©2025).
    - *Claims & Insights:* Highlights industry benchmarks (e.g., Genshin's cross-platform tech, Wuthering Waves' ACT combat, Love and Deepspace's 3D otome presentation), identifies common pain points (gacha fatigue, late-game repetition, optimization issues, localization adjustments), and notes successful retention strategies (generous F2P economies, roguelike integration, stable update cadences).
    - *Definitions/Procedures:* Defines genre-specific mechanics and terms: "长草期" (content drought), "集成战略" (roguelike mode), "合轴" (synergy trigger), "声骸" (echo progression), "饭圈化" (fandom dynamics), "Companionship" design, Burst combat loops, Synchronizer systems, and gacha pity/guarantee structures. Outlines progression procedures (gacha → star-up → material farming) and combat input/optimization steps.
    - *Evidence/Visuals:* Supported by DianDian Data's independent market research, featuring trend charts/tables for revenue, MAU, retention, demographics, and ASO keywords. Includes structured scoring rubrics, weighted category evaluations, and qualitative gameplay/community analysis.
    - *Check against constraints:* Mention topics, entities, dates, tables, figures, definitions, procedures, claims, metrics, evidence types. Do not invent facts. Return only summary text.
